condition: For the cross-fitted propensity estimator used in def:feasible-erm and each analysis clip q, with ehat_q(x)=min{1-q,max{q,ehat(x)}}, there is a constant C_v such that, conditionally on the training folds, e_P(x)/ehat_q(x)^2 + (1-e_P(x))/(1-ehat_q(x))^2 <= C_v/max{p_P(x),q} for P_X-almost every x.
reason: The feasible upper proof uses the inverse-propensity conditional variance scale Var(Gamma_q|X)<=C/max{p,q}. Bounded clipped scores alone give only the cruder envelope scale q^{-2}; the variance scale requires the estimated clipped propensity not to be much smaller than the true clipped propensity on evaluation points.
standard_or_novel: standard: overlap-compatible / relatively stable propensity weights, implied for example by a uniform relative-consistency condition ehat_q asymp min{1-q,max{q,e_P}} on the analysis folds
not_crux: This condition controls the stochastic empirical-process variance of the feasible score. It does not assert the margin-overlap lower-bound calibration, the regret exponent, or the nuisance drift balance; those are still derived in the proof.
